Gdelt._comment: GDELT DOC 2.0 news collector (third-party attention). Only read when 'news' is in Collectors. Timespan is a GDELT window token (1w/2w/1m). GDELT allows ~1 request/5s per IP, so the collector is strictly sequential and paces requests by InterRequestDelaySeconds; the value is only validated non-negative, but 6+ is recommended to stay under that limit (the default is 6). On HTTP 429 the reader retries MaxRetriesOn429 times with exponential backoff starting at RetryBackoffSeconds (60 -> 60s/120s).

Ai._comment: AI chat-client seam (Microsoft.Extensions.AI IChatClient) + the filing analyzer + the directional filing signal source that consume it. DISABLED by default: a blank Provider means no AI is wired and no provider packages load, so no earnings release is ever read and the deterministic Neutral GuidanceChange (spec 57) stands. Directional filing signals (a beat lifts Trajectory via a Positive GuidanceChange, a miss lowers it via Negative) require AI ENABLED via either: Ollama (keyless/local — set Provider 'ollama', Model to an installed tag e.g. 'llama3.1', ensure it is pulled and running at Ollama:Endpoint) or Anthropic (set Provider 'anthropic', Model e.g. 'claude-opus-4-8', and Anthropic:ApiKey or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). No model or key is hard-coded. When AI is enabled the source also needs a compliant Radar:Sec:UserAgent to fetch the EX-99.1 body. MaxInputLength caps the earnings-release characters sent to the analyzer; MinConfidence gates the directional read (below it -> no signal); MaxFilingsPerRun caps how many filings are analyzed per run.
